description:
Offered: every other year
This course introduces students to the processes driving economic activity over space. From local to global economies, students will identify patterns and trends that explain the spatial distribution of economic resources, activities, and institutions. Students will use spatial economic models to explore a variety of economic issues including economic development, environmental economics, global business, and political economy, among others. Cross-listed with ECON 3100
Meets General Education Requirement(s): PL
